dialogueChunks.intro: It's over, {player_name}. The weight I've been carrying... I can finally set it down.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We held a ceremony at sunset. {quest_stakes} placed a stone on the memorial cairn. {target_npc} spoke for the community, and there wasn't a dry eye. They said the dead can rest now because someone cared enough to {quest_action}. My brother's stone is at the top. I put it there myself.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this. It's from all the families. Every one of them wanted you to know that what you did mattered.

playerResponses.text: Thirty stones. I'll carry their memory with me.
I hope the ceremony brought some peace. Your brother deserved it.

dialogueChunks.intro: I thought vengeance would feel like fire. Instead, it feels like rain after a drought. Quiet. Necessary.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} returned the personal belongings you recovered to each family. A mother held her son's ring and wept for an hour. These objects won't bring anyone back, but they close a chapter. {quest_threat} who collected them like prizes are gone, and {quest_stakes} can start to heal.
dialogueChunks.outro: Here. This was set aside for the one who helped us {quest_action}. You did more than avenge: you restored.

playerResponses.text: Returning what was stolen felt more important than the fighting.
I'm glad the families have something to hold onto.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. I need to be honest with you about something.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I expected to feel triumph when you told me you'd managed to {quest_action}. I didn't. I felt hollow. {target_npc} warned me about this: said that revenge fills the hole with something, but not with what you lost. My daughter is still gone. But {quest_stakes} are safe now, and {quest_threat} can't hurt anyone else. That has to be enough.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this. And if anyone asks whether vengeance is worth it, tell them the truth: it's not satisfaction, but it's closure.

playerResponses.text: Closure is worth something. I hope you find your way forward.
Your daughter was brave. Carry that forward, not the grief.

dialogueChunks.intro: The elders called an assembly this morning. What they decided took everyone by surprise.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They've declared an annual remembrance day for {quest_stakes}. Not a day of anger: a day of memory. {target_npc} suggested it, and the vote was unanimous. Your name will be spoken at the first ceremony as the one who helped {quest_action}. The farms are being replanted, the fences rebuilt, and for the first time in months, people are talking about the future.
dialogueChunks.outro: We set this aside before you even returned. Some things you just know will be earned. Take it with our gratitude.

playerResponses.text: A day of memory, not anger. That's the right way to honor them.
Rebuild well. I'll think of this place when the remembrance day comes.
